summary: Matched a batch of tournament/game-mode helper functions in src/melee/gm/gm_18A5.c, replacing many placeholder declarations with typed C implementations and more precise prototypes. The PR also split local static data and inferred structs into gm_18A5.static.h, added required baselib/gm/lb/mn includes, corrected gm_80164430 and mnStageSel_8025BBD4 return types for call-site matching, and implemented object/camera/light/model/audio/archive setup helpers used by the tournament scene path.
what_changed: Implemented numerous previously stubbed gm_18A5 functions, including fn_8018A970, fn_8018EC48, fn_8018EC7C, fn_8018F4A0, fn_8018F5F0, fn_8018F640, fn_8018F674, fn_8018F6A8, fn_8018F6DC, fn_8018F6FC, fn_8018F71C, fn_8018F74C, fn_8018F808, fn_8018FDC4, fn_8018FF9C, fn_80190174, fn_801901F8, fn_8019027C, fn_8019035C, fn_8019044C, fn_80190480, fn_80192690, fn_80193230, and gm_801963B4_OnEnter.

Moved static data and inferred local structs from gm_18A5.c into a new gm_18A5.static.h file, including lbl_804D4190, archive pointers lbl_804D6638 through lbl_804D6648, scene pointers lbl_804D664C and lbl_804D6650, lbl_80473AB8 array layout, gm_804771C4, and lbl_803D9D20_t.

Expanded gm_18A5.c includes from a minimal types include to specific dependencies for controller, fog, gobj, gobjgxlink, gobjobject, gobjproc, jobj, gm_unsplit, gmmain_lib, lb_00F9, lbarchive, lbaudio_ax, lblanguage, mnmain, and mnstagesel.

Updated gm_18A5.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures where evidence from matched implementations allowed it, such as HSD_GObj callbacks, HSD_JObj transform helpers, CObj descriptor loaders, DynamicModelDesc model setup, and typed integer/float return helpers.
Changed gm_80164430 in gm_1601.h from an unknown prototype to bool gm_80164430(u16), matching its use as a stage-number validator in fn_8018F4A0.
Changed mnStageSel_8025BBD4 return type from u8 to int in both mnstagesel.c and mnstagesel.h so fn_8018F4A0 can use the random stage result in an int temp and printf/assert path.

Added tournament scene OnEnter logic that loads audio state, archives GmTou1p, GmTou2p, MnExtAll, and TmBox.dat, initializes SIS tournament data using language-dependent SdTou.usd/SdTou.dat, calls fn_801935B8, and starts audio playback/setup.

Implemented reusable object creation helpers for cameras, lights, fog, and dynamic models using GObj_Create, HSD_CObjLoadDesc, HSD_JObjLoadJoint, HSD_GObjObject_80390A70, GObj_SetupGXLink, and GObj_SetupGXLinkMax.
smart_moves: Isolated file-local globals and inferred static structs in gm_18A5.static.h, keeping gm_18A5.c focused on function bodies while preserving static linkage and exact local data layout.
Used concrete return and parameter types in headers only where the matched bodies justified them, reducing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS noise and making later decompilation of callers easier.

Matched small wrapper/helper functions first, such as clamping wrappers around gm_801A36A0/gm_801A36C0/gm_801A3680 and simple index conversion helpers, which likely unlocked larger surrounding functions.
Used #pragma dont_inline around functions where inlining behavior mattered for matching, specifically fn_8018F5F0, fn_80192690, and fn_80193230.
Preserved assert/printf behavior in fn_8018F4A0, including the exact impossible-stage diagnostic string and gmtoulib.c assertion metadata, rather than simplifying the validation branch.
Replaced an M2C_FIELD access in fn_8018F62C with the structured HSD_GObj user_data field, improving readability while relying on known baselib struct layout.
Encoded sentinel transform behavior in fn_8018FDC4 and fn_8018FF9C using 666.0F checks before setting individual translate/scale axes, preserving the original partial-update convention.
Adjusted nearby dependent prototypes outside gm_18A5, especially mnStageSel_8025BBD4 and gm_80164430, instead of forcing casts at the call site.
decomp_lessons: When a matched function reveals an external API's real type, update the owning header and implementation together; this PR did that for mnStageSel_8025BBD4 and gm_80164430.
For scene setup code, exact GObj kind/class/prio/link arguments are part of the match and should be retained as constants until better names are available.
Language-dependent asset selection can require a non-inlined helper returning literal filenames; here fn_8018F5F0 returns SdTou.usd for saved US language and SdTou.dat otherwise.
Known library struct fields should replace raw M2C_FIELD macros when the offset is confidently understood, as with HSD_GObj::user_data.

Static data layout often needs extra inferred padding fields before functions can match; gm_18A5.static.h documents offsets such as lbl_803D9D20_t.cobj at 0xB0, xD0 at 0xD0, eye_position at 0xD8, and interest at 0xEC.
Small signedness and width choices can affect matching and call-site codegen; changing mnStageSel_8025BBD4 from u8 to int was paired with an int temp in fn_8018F4A0.
Use #pragma push/pop with dont_inline sparingly to match compiler behavior around simple helpers or setup functions that the compiler might otherwise inline differently.
Menu/game-mode decomp often depends on cross-system includes from baselib, lbarchive, lbaudio, language, and menu headers; precise includes are better than relying on placeholder types.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions retain address-based names such as fn_8018F4A0 and fn_8019035C.
Game-mode exported functions use the gm_ address prefix, such as gm_8018F634 and gm_801963B4_OnEnter.
Lifecycle handlers keep suffixes like _OnEnter, as in gm_801963B4_OnEnter.
Static labels keep lbl_ address names, such as lbl_80473AB8, lbl_804D6638, lbl_804D664C, and lbl_803D9D20.
Inferred struct fields use offset-style names such as x0, x1, x32, x5E, xD0, and xD4 when semantic names are not yet known.
Padding fields use offset-based pad names such as pad2, pad33, pad5F, pad_0, pad_B4, and pad_E4.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added #pragma dont_inline blocks around specific functions to preserve expected code generation.
Used PAD_STACK(4) in fn_80193230, indicating stack-shape adjustment was needed for the match.
Matched validation flow in fn_8018F4A0 with printf followed by __assert when gm_80164430 rejects the stage value.
Implemented clamp-to-4 wrappers before calling gm_801A36A0, gm_801A36C0, and gm_801A3680, preserving branch structure and argument conversion.
Used explicit casts such as (u8) arg0 and (u8) i where needed for generated loads/indexing.
Kept sentinel comparisons as integer casts of float arguments against 666 or 0, matching the observed control flow in transform and FOV helpers.
Used exact object creation/link setup constants, including GObj_Create class/prio parameters, gxlink priority values, and gxlink_prios assignments.
Introduced a static header for local data so struct layouts and global symbol declarations could support matching without exposing them as public API.
follow_up_queries: Search later gm_18A5 PRs for semantic names replacing lbl_80473AB8 fields x0, x1, x32, and x5E.
Check whether gm_18A5.static.h static data was later moved into a broader tournament-mode header or renamed with real struct types.
Search for follow-up matches of fn_8018A514, fn_8018AA74, fn_8018B090, fn_8018E46C, fn_801910E0, fn_80191154, fn_801935B8, and gm_801964A4_OnLeave.
Look for later comments or commits explaining the 666.0F sentinel used by fn_8018FDC4 and fn_8018FF9C.
Search for usages of mnStageSel_8025BBD4 after its return type changed from u8 to int to confirm no other call sites needed adjustment.
